user.title: Set up IDM Navigator Heat Pump
user.description:
[Need Help? Read the Documentation]({wiki_url})

Enter the connection details of your IDM heat pump.

**Prerequisite:** Modbus TCP must be enabled in the IDM controller under *Installer level → Communication → Modbus*.

data_description.name: Display name for the device in Home Assistant, e.g. "IDM Heat Pump".
data_description.host: IP address or hostname of your IDM Navigator 2.0, e.g. `192.168.1.100`.
data_description.port: Modbus TCP port of the controller. Default is `502`.
data_description.slave_id: Modbus slave ID. Default is `1`. Only change this if multiple devices share the same bus.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure – {name}
reconfigure.description:
[Need Help? Read the Documentation]({wiki_url})

Current connection: **{host}**

Update the network settings of your heat pump. The integration will restart automatically afterwards.

data_description.host: New IP address or hostname of the IDM Navigator 2.0.

options.title: Configuration – {name}
options.description: Adjust the polling interval and installed components. Changes will restart the integration.

data_description.scan_interval: How often to read data from the heat pump (in seconds). Shorter intervals mean faster updates but more network traffic.
data_description.hide_unused_registers: Automatically mark sensors that return inactive values (e.g. −1.0) as *unavailable* and hide them.
data_description.heating_circuits: Select all heating circuits that are installed and active in your system. Circuit A is always active.
data_description.zone_count: Number of IDM zone modules installed (0 if none).
data_description.technician_codes: Exposes two sensors showing the current Installer Level codes (Level 1 and Level 2). Codes are refreshed every minute.
data_description.enable_cascade: Enables reading and writing cascade operation registers. Only enable this if you have a cascaded heat pump setup.

zones.title: Configure zone modules
zones.description: Enter the number of active rooms per zone module (maximum 8 rooms per zone). Configuring {zone_count} zone module(s).

data_description.zone_0_rooms: Number of active room sensors in Zone 1 (1–8).
data_description.zone_1_rooms: Number of active room sensors in Zone 2 (1–8).
data_description.zone_2_rooms: Number of active room sensors in Zone 3 (1–8).
data_description.zone_3_rooms: Number of active room sensors in Zone 4 (1–8).
data_description.zone_4_rooms: Number of active room sensors in Zone 5 (1–8).
data_description.zone_5_rooms: Number of active room sensors in Zone 6 (1–8).
data_description.zone_6_rooms: Number of active room sensors in Zone 7 (1–8).
data_description.zone_7_rooms: Number of active room sensors in Zone 8 (1–8).
data_description.zone_8_rooms: Number of active room sensors in Zone 9 (1–8).
data_description.zone_9_rooms: Number of active room sensors in Zone 10 (1–8).

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to the heat pump. Check the IP address, port, and whether Modbus TCP is enabled in the controller.
error.host_required: Please enter a valid IP address or hostname.
error.name_required: Please enter a name for the device.

abort.already_configured: This heat pump is already configured.
abort.reconfigure_successful: Connection details updated. The integration will restart.

no_device_configured.message: No IDM heat pump is currently configured or loaded.

invalid_mode.message: Invalid operating mode: "{mode}". Valid modes: standby, automatik, automatic, abwesend, away, urlaub, holiday, nur warmwasser, hot water only, nur heizung/kuehlung, heating/cooling only.

acknowledge_risk_required.message: You must set acknowledge_risk to true to use the write_register service.

invalid_datatype.message: Invalid datatype: "{datatype}". Valid values: uint16, int16, float, uchar, bool.

cannot_connect.title: Cannot connect to IDM heat pump
cannot_connect.description:
Home Assistant cannot reach the IDM heat pump at **{host}**.

Please check:
- Network connectivity to the device
- Modbus TCP is enabled in the controller (*Installer level → Communication → Modbus*)
- The IP address and port are correct in the integration settings
